rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Under Civ. Proc. 583.210, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 1, DEFENDANT MCGUIRE AND HESTER Motion To Dismiss Under Civ. Proc. 583.210. Granted per Code of Civil Procedure sections 583.210 and 583.250. Defendant was not properly served within the mandatory three-year time period. A general appearance by the Defendant after the three-year period does not cure the defective service or preclude Defendant from seeking dismissal. (Brookview Condominium Owners' Association v. Heltzer Enterprises, (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 502; Busching v. Superior Court, (1974) 12 Cal.3d 44.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 2, DEFENDANT MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INCC., A CALIFORNIA, LHJS INVESTMENTS, FORM UNKNOWN, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, A CALIFORNIA, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, A CALIFORNIA, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third causes of action and off calendar to the extent the demurrer is directed to the second cause of action. Plaintiff has not alleged facts showing that an injustice would result if the separate existence of the moving defendants is respected. Plaintiff has alleged that an injustice might result in the future, but this insufficient to pierce the corporate veil. Moreover, plaintiff seeks reverse piercing, which is not available in California. (Postal Instant Press, Inc. v. Kasawa Corp. (2008) 162 Cal. App. 4th 1510). Amendment is most unlikely to cure these defects. Moving defendants are not named in the second cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRIBECA COMPANIES, LLC VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel: 1) Production Of Documents; 2) Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; And 3) Further Responses To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 4, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Compel: 1) Production Of Documents; 2) Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; And 3) Further Responses To Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BRADLEY VS. HWA 555 OWNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Teminating, Issue, And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 19, DEFENDANT HWA 555 OWNERS LLC, VORNADO REALTY TRUST Motion For TeRminating, Issue, and Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoilation Of Evidence. The recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg is adopted in its entirety, as follows: Denied. As to terminating sanctions, the record before the Court does not come close to supporting such an extreme sanction. As to defendants' request for issue and evidentiary sanctions under the Discovery Act, absent a prior court order compelling discovery, such sanctions are only available in "exceptional circumstances" to remedy "misconduct" that is "sufficiently egregious." See New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal. App. 4th 1403, 1426, 1428 (2009). Here, there is no prior court order, thus, issue and evidentiary sanctions are not proper absent such "exceptional circumstances." The record here lacks any such "exceptional circumstances," which typically involve some variety of willful or intentional misconduct. See id. at 1428-1430 (discussing "exceptional circumstances") The record does not support a conclusion that the right boot was discarded "in anticipation of a discovery request." Cedars-Sinai Medical Center v. Superior Court, 18 Cal. 4th 1, 12 (1998). The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN LOW et al VS. SUSANNE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 14, CROSS DEFENDANT EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO, JENNIFER HOVLAND MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the prayer for injunctive relief and denied as to the references to the 25 bank accounts. The relief sought in the prayer for injunctive relief goes well beyond what is available for an injunction to abate a nuisance, which is the only basis that Susanne says she is seeking injunctive relief. Without additional facts, it is not possible to determine whether Susanne's allegations as to any or all of the alleged 25 bank accounts must be raised in the probate division or may be asserted in a civil action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BENTLY RESERVE, L.P., A CALIFORIA LIMITED et al VS. ANDREAS G. PAPALIOLIOS, AN INDIVIDUAL, AKA "SAL R" et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Special Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike By Deft Andreas G. Papaliolios
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JULY 17, 2012, LINE 23, DEFENDANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS' Special Motion To Strike. Denied in its entirety. As to the first cause of action for libel, Mr. Papaliolios satisfied his burden of showing that non-incidental allegations of the claim arise out of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, but plaintiffs also satisfied their burden of providing evidence showing that there is requisite minimal merit to this claim. As to the second and third causes of action, Mr. Papaliolios did not satisfy his burden of showing that non-incidental allegations of either of these claims arise out of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
